behaviors.description: Evaluate-stage command stores grounding into dedicated `grounding` artifact, not overwriting `plan` artifact
behaviors.justification: The failure is a code-level decision about which attachment key to write to. A unit test mocking the artifact storage mechanism can verify that evaluate calls the write method with key `grounding` (and not `plan`). No cross-module wiring or real I/O is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test that mocks the attachment storage write operation. Call the evaluate stage with test inputs, then assert that write(key='grounding', ...) is called with the appropriate grounding data, and write(key='plan', ...) is never called during evaluate.

behaviors.description: Implement-stage flow reads grounding when available and writes execution output to `plan` artifact
behaviors.justification: The failure is about accessing the correct artifact slots. A unit test with mocked artifact read/write can verify that implement calls read(key='grounding') and write(key='plan'). The mock ensures the code paths are exercised without requiring real artifact infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test mocking artifact read and write operations. Set up the mock read to return test grounding data. Call implement stage. Assert read(key='grounding') is called once, assert write(key='plan', ...) is called once with valid execution output, and assert the grounding data was actually used in the plan generation.

behaviors.description: Deep-dive no longer overwrites `plan` artifact as redundant side effect when diagnosis already exists in issue content
behaviors.justification: This is conditional logic—deep-dive should skip writing to `plan` when a diagnosis already exists. A unit test mocking the artifact write operation can verify the condition is correctly implemented by asserting the write method is not called with key `plan` in the relevant scenario.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test mocking the attachment storage write operation. Call deep-dive with issue content that already contains a diagnosis. Assert that write(key='plan', ...) is never called during this execution. Verify that other artifacts may be written (e.g., updated diagnosis), but the `plan` slot is specifically skipped.

behaviors.description: In full multi-step agent run (deep-dive → evaluate → implement), final implementation plan reflects grounding produced earlier in the run
behaviors.justification: The failure—grounding not persisting through the pipeline—cannot be caught by unit tests of individual skills in isolation. It is a cross-module artifact flow concern. The failure boundary is at the integration of multiple skills sharing a common artifact storage mechanism. You must wire all three skills together with a consistent artifact storage (mocked or in-memory) to verify grounding persists and flows from evaluate through to implement.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test orchestrating the full three-skill pipeline (deep-dive → evaluate → implement) with a shared test artifact storage (in-memory store or carefully wired mock storage accessible to all skills). Inject a test case, run the pipeline, verify that evaluate produces a `grounding` artifact with concrete diagnostic information, verify that implement reads this `grounding`, and verify the final `plan` artifact reflects or incorporates that grounding information.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the minimum because grounding persistence across skill boundaries is a cross-module property. Unit tests of individual skills would verify they read/write to correct keys, but would not catch the failure where grounding is lost in the transition between skills. Only running the full pipeline with shared artifact storage can verify end-to-end flow.

behaviors.description: When grounding is absent, implement falls back safely and emits valid plan artifact without corrupting other stored artifacts
behaviors.justification: This is error-handling and fallback logic within the implement skill. A unit test mocking artifact read to return None can verify that the fallback is correctly implemented. The mock isolates the error path without requiring real infrastructure or cross-module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test mocking the artifact read operation to return None (simulating absent grounding). Call implement stage with typical inputs. Assert: (a) no exception is raised, (b) write(key='plan', ...) is called with a valid fallback plan, (c) write method is not called with corrupt or invalid data, (d) no unexpected writes to other artifacts occur as a side effect of the missing grounding.
